data.text: The failing server test is asserting that a `refs/ddx/iterations/...` ref exists, so I’m checking whether this repo simply lacks the expected fixtures or whether the test setup is supposed to create the ref and didn’t. If it’s the former, I’ll record it as an external blocker; if it’s the latter, I may be able to unstick the suite without touching the GraphQL code.

data.text: I’ve hit a separate repository test failure that looks like a real bug in the land-orchestrator path, not in GraphQL. I’m doing one quick source read to confirm whether it’s something I can safely fix in this pass; if not, I’ll stop with a `needs_investigation` rationale instead of forcing a risky cross-scope edit.

I’ve confirmed the GraphQL deadcode condition is clean. The remaining blocker is an unrelated server test failure in the land-orchestrator path, so I’m recording that as `needs_investigation` and will stop without forcing a risky cross-scope change.

I verified the five bead symbols are no longer present in the current `cli/internal/server/graphql` tree and recorded that as DELETE in [decisions.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4c5beab2-20260506T193554-4c7a6e00/.ddx/executions/20260506T193554-4c7a6e00/decisions.md).

Validation results:
- `cd cli && go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./... | rg "internal/server/graphql"` returned no matches.
- `cd cli && go test ./...` is not green because of an unrelated failure in [workers_gates_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4c5beab2-20260506T193554-4c7a6e00/cli/internal/server/workers_gates_test.go#L252), where `refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-gates-fail/20260506T193752Z-c5e29ea40957` is missing.

I wrote the blocker note in [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4c5beab2-20260506T193554-4c7a6e00/.ddx/executions/20260506T193554-4c7a6e00/no_changes_rationale.txt). No commit was made.

If you want, I can investigate the `workers_gates_test` failure next.
